title: olea europaea subsp. maroccana
intro: Olea europaea subsp. maroccana, also known as Moroccan olive tree, is a subspecies of the olive tree (Olea europaea) native to the Atlas Mountains in Morocco. This plant is rich in medicinal properties, particularly its essential oil, which contains compounds like oleocanthal and oleuropein, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Olea europaea subsp. maroccana include reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health, as well as having antimicrobial and antifungal properties, which can aid in wound healing and protecting against infections. In traditional herbal preparations, the leaves and branches of the Moroccan olive tree are typically used to make teas, infusions, and topical creams, which can be used to soothe skin irritations and promote overall well-being.
